title: राहु दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राहु दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ रां राहवे नमः

राहुदोषनिवारणार्थं स्तोत्रं प्रवक्ष्यामि शुभम्।
राहुग्रहं प्रशान्त्यर्थं भक्त्या शृणुत सादरम्॥१॥

अर्धकायं महावीर्यं चन्द्रादित्यविमर्दनम्।
सिंहिकागर्भसम्भूतं तं राहुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥२॥

नीलवर्णं किरीटिनं चतुर्बाहुं भयंकरम्।
सिंहासनगतं राहुं सर्पराजं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

छायाग्रहं महाकायं मायाविनं सुरारिणम्।
राहुं प्रशान्तिमापन्नं सौम्यरूपं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

राहुदोषो विनश्येत राहुपीडा लयं गता।
राहुग्रहप्रसादेन सर्वकार्यं प्रसिध्यति॥५॥

दुर्गा देवी महाशक्तिः राहुदोषनिवारिणी।
दुर्गां नमामि सततं राहुशान्तिकरीं शुभाम्॥६॥

सर्पदोषविनाशाय राहुदोषनिवारणम्।
यः पठेत् शनिवासरे राहुदोषो विनश्यति॥७॥

॥ इति श्री राहु दोष निवारण स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram ||

Om Ram Rahave Namah

Rahu-dosha-nivaranartham Stotram Pravakshyami Shubham.
Rahu-graham Prashanti-artham Bhaktya Shrinuta Sadaram.||1||

Ardha-kayam Maha-viryam Chandra-aditya-vimardanam.
Simhika-garbha-sambhutam Tam Rahum Pranamami-aham.||2||

Nila-varnam Kiritinam Chatur-bahum Bhayankaram.
Simhasana-gatam Rahum Sarpa-rajam Namamyaham.||3||

Chhaya-graham Maha-kayam Maya-vinam Sura-arinam.
Rahum Prashantim-apannam Saumya-rupam Namamyaham.||4||

Rahu-dosho Vinashyeta Rahu-pida Layam Gata.
Rahu-graha-prasadena Sarva-karyam Prasidhyati.||5||

Durga Devi Maha-shaktih Rahu-dosha-nivarini.
Durgam Namami Satatam Rahu-shanti-karim Shubham.||6||

Sarpa-dosha-vinashaya Rahu-dosha-nivaranam.
Yah Pathet Shani-vasare Rahu-dosho Vinashyati.||7||

|| Iti Shri Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: राहुदोष निवारण हेतु यह स्तोत्र। अर्धकाय, महावीर्य, चन्द्र-सूर्य को ग्रसने वाले, सिंहिका गर्भ से उत्पन्न राहु को प्रणाम। नीलवर्ण, किरीटधारी, चतुर्बाहु, सिंहासनगत सर्पराज राहु को नमन। छायाग्रह, महाकाय, मायावी राहु को प्रशान्त सौम्यरूप में नमन। राहुदोष नष्ट हो, राहुपीड़ा शान्त हो। दुर्गा देवी राहुदोषनिवारिणी हैं। शनिवार को पाठ करने से राहुदोष नष्ट होता है।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of rahu dosha and rahu mahadasha afflictions
Pacifies the shadow planet Rahu for astrological relief
Invokes Durga Devi as the primary Rahu-dosha remover
Traditionally recited for removal of confusion, deception, and sudden misfortunes caused by rahu
Best recited on Saturdays during Rahu Kaal
Beneficial during Rahu Mahadasha and Antardasha periods
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram (राहु दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Graha Shanti Vidhi and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to beneficial during Rahu Mahadasha and Antardasha periods and pacify the shadow planet Rahu for astrological relief. It is also said to remove Rahu Dosha and Rahu Mahadasha afflictions. This prayer is traditionally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) to invoke divine grace. Comprising 7 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer to remove Rahu affliction and Rahu Mahadasha problems.
relatedSearches: Rahu dosh nivaran stotram lyrics Sanskrit
Rahu beej mantra Om Bhram Bhreem Bhroum
Kaal Sarp dosh nivaran mantra Trimbakeshwar
Rahu dosh nivaran stotram Hindi PDF

seoFaqs.question: What is Rahu Dosh and why does this stotram exist?
seoFaqs.answer: In Vedic astrology, Rahu Dosh refers to the affliction caused when the shadow planet Rahu sits in malefic placement in a horoscope, often producing sudden financial loss, recurring health issues, illusions and obstacles in marriage and career. As Rudraksha-Ratna's Rahu Dosha article and AstroAhead's mantra-jaap page explain, traditional remedies combine yantra worship, gemstone wearing and the regular recitation of Rahu-specific stotrams and bija mantras such as 'Om Bhram Bhreem Bhroum Sah Rahave Namah' or 'Om Raam Rahave Namah'. The Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram exists as a longer praise hymn that, unlike the bija mantra alone, narrates Rahu's mythological origin, his 25 names from the Vamadeva Rishi list, and the specific afflictions he is asked to lift.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the underlying Rahu Stotra used for Rahu Dosh Nivaran?
seoFaqs.answer: Astromantra and AstroAhead both attribute the core Rahu Stotra to the sage Vamadeva, listing twenty-five names of Rahu used to praise the shadow graha for the relief of his devotees. The Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram preserves this nucleus and is generally chanted alongside the standard Navagraha framework, so a single sitting will often combine the bija mantra of Rahu, the Vamadeva twenty-five-name stotra, and a closing prayer for shanti. Lineages working at Tarapith and Trimbakeshwar add further regional verses for Rahu and Ketu together because the two shadow grahas are treated as a karmic pair in Kaal Sarp dosha contexts, where Rahu and Ketu form the head and tail of the cosmic serpent.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does tradition attribute to chanting the Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The chiefly cited benefits, summarised by Rudraksha-Ratna and AstroAhead, are relief from sudden illness, easing of mental unrest and confusion (a Rahu signature), restoration of stalled marriages, reduction of financial losses caused by deception, and strengthening of confidence and clarity of purpose. Spiritually the stotram is said to cultivate fearlessness, self-realisation and contentment, helping the chanter align with their karmic path rather than fight Rahu's transits in resistance. Because Rahu rules things hidden, foreign and unconventional, devotees in technology, foreign trade, aviation and research often chant the stotram before major decisions during Rahu mahadasha or antardasha periods.

seoFaqs.question: When and how often should the Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is the day astrologically attributed to Shani and Rahu work, so the stotram is most commonly recited on Saturdays and during Rahu Kalam each day, as recorded by AstroAhead and Rudraksha-Ratna. Traditional sankalpas range from 21 days for mild dosha, to 40 days (mandala) for sustained relief, to 108 days for serious afflictions during a Rahu mahadasha. The standard practice is to bathe, sit on a dark blue or black asana facing west or south, light a mustard-oil or sesame-oil lamp, and chant the stotram either 11, 21 or 108 times, often combined with a prescribed number of Rahu beej mantra repetitions and an offering of black sesame and water to a peepal tree.

seoFaqs.question: Is the Rahu Dosh Nivaran Stotram a substitute for a full Rahu Shanti homam?
seoFaqs.answer: It is a complementary practice, not strictly a substitute. As Rudraksha-Ratna's Rahu Dosh Nivaran Puja page and the temple-based Rahu Shanti pages from Himalaya Vedic World and Yatradham explain, severe Rahu doshas, especially those identified during a major mahadasha or as part of Kaal Sarp yoga, are best addressed with a full Rahu Shanti homam involving 11,000 to 72,000 mantra repetitions and prescribed havan dravya. The daily stotram-and-mantra practice carries significant spiritual benefit and is what most householders use, but it is generally treated as upachara that prepares and sustains the homam rather than replacing it. An astrologer's reading of the chart usually decides which of the two paths, or what combination, is appropriate.

seoFaqs.question: Which symptoms in a chart suggest a need to chant this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Astrological consultations, as summarised by Rudraksha-Ratna and AstroAhead, flag several Rahu-affliction signatures: Rahu placed in the 1st, 2nd, 5th, 7th or 8th house with malefic aspect, Rahu-Ketu axis along the 1-7 axis (a Kaal Sarp pattern), Rahu conjunct Sun, Moon or Mars, and ongoing Rahu mahadasha or antardasha during a difficult life period. Subjectively, devotees are advised to consider the stotram when they experience repeated unexplained losses, sudden mental confusion, recurring nightmares, addiction patterns, and stalled marriages or careers despite effort. The stotram is also used preventively before major Rahu transits and at the start of new ventures involving foreign travel, technology or unconventional fields traditionally ruled by the shadow graha.
